artifacts.claimText: The University of Bologna policy treats GenAI as permitted support for teaching, learning, research and knowledge valorisation, while stating that GenAI should serve people and not replace creative and intellectual activity.
artifacts.normalizedValue: GenAI permitted as responsible support, not replacement

citation.citationTitle: University Policy for an Ethical and Responsible Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Teaching and Research
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: University Policy for an Ethical and Responsible Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Teaching and Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The Policy guidelines aim to ensure that GenAI is used in a responsible and conscious way; to help improve the quality of teaching, learning and research also in collaborations with society and businesses; at the service of the people and not to replace their creative and intellectual activity.

artifacts.claimText: University of Bologna community members covered by the teaching and research GenAI policy are responsible for not entering third-party personal data or confidential/protected information into online GenAI systems unless an appropriate legal basis, consent, or confidentiality assurance exists.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Personal data and confidential information restricted in online GenAI systems

artifacts.sourceTitle: University Policy for an Ethical and Responsible Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Teaching and Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Refraining from providing online GenAI systems with personal data of third parties, including special categories of data such as health or genetic data, unless the data subject gives his or her consent or another suitable legal basis for the processing exists. Refraining from uploading confidential information into GenAI systems, including protected or confidential data

artifacts.claimText: For GenAI use in assessed student work such as dissertation structure, dissertation writing, or programming code, University of Bologna guidance says the uses comply with policy principles only with active supervision, critical thinking, accountability for results, citation where required, and checking with the teacher whether the use is permitted.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Assessed-work GenAI use conditioned on supervision, accountability, citation, and teacher permission checks

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It complies with the University Policy principles, provided you implement active supervision and critical thinking and are able to account for the generated results. artifacts.claimText: For GenAI use in student learning, University of Bologna guidance says researching/explaining concepts, querying with examples, and summarising books or articles comply with policy principles and generally do not require citation, while students should check generated information, references and sources for accuracy.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Learning uses comply with policy principles with source and accuracy checks

artifacts.sourceTitle: Use cases of generative artificial intelligence in student learning
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Researching and explaining concepts, theories, topics. It complies with the University Policy principles. It does not require citing the use of GenAI. Check the generated information for accuracy. Closely check all references to cited sources. Check the output relating to non-established knowledge for reliability. Check the sources used for accuracy also by means of specialist search engines.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Researching existing knowledge and generating ideas

artifacts.title: University of Bologna staged GenAI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Official University of Bologna sources provide university-level GenAI policy and guidance for teaching, research, student learning, assessed work, citation/transparency, and privacy/confidentiality responsibilities. Claims are staged as agent-reviewed candidates only.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims rely on official public University of Bologna sources retrieved on 2026-05-14 and do not publish canonical public claims.
Course and programme notices were rejected in favor of primary university-level policy and guidance sources.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
